steps.title: Place the contrast
steps.content: The first independent clause says the bright object began to dim after a few weeks. However can close that clause as a parenthetical contrast word, and a semicolon can then introduce the general explanation about short-lived supernovas.

steps.title: Reject weaker punctuation
steps.content: B splits the thought into a separate sentence and stacks however with although. C creates a comma splice between independent clauses. D also starts the second clause with however followed by although, making the contrast redundant. The remaining choice keeps the clauses connected correctly. The answer is A.
